articles.title: Dune and Avengers Movies to Open on Same Day
articles.summary: A new movie called Dune: Part Three is coming out in December 2026. It is the last part of a sci-fi series starring Timothée Chalamet and Zendaya. The trailer shows big space battles and follows the story of Paul Atreides. But there is a problem: another big movie, Avengers: Doomsday, is set to open on the same day. Avengers movies usually make a lot of money, so studios try to avoid this. Dune has already booked special Imax screens for its opening weekend, which means it might not change its date. Warner Bros, the studio behind Dune, released the trailer after winning Oscars. They are showing they are strong even though their company might be sold. The first Dune movie made $402 million, and the second made even more. Fans call this battle 'Dundesday,' but the dates could still change.

articles.title: Toby Samuel's Big Tennis Dream
articles.summary: Toby Samuel is a 23-year-old tennis player from England who has worked hard to become a professional. In just one year, he moved from being ranked 1,405th in the world to 171st, which is a huge jump. He won seven singles titles in six months, making him feel like a real pro. Toby trains at Bath University and credits his success to staying healthy and practicing well. He missed part of 2024 due to an injury but recovered in 2025. Now, he wants to keep winning and play in Grand Slam tournaments, which are the biggest events in tennis. He is excited to share his journey with his family and supporters. His next goal is to play in the main draw of a Grand Slam singles event, something he has never done before.

articles.title: A Tennis Star Speaks Out About a Tournament
articles.summary: Aryna Sabalenka is a top tennis player who won big tournaments like the Australian Open. She recently decided not to play in a tournament in Dubai because she needed to rest and stay healthy. The tournament director criticized her for withdrawing, which made Sabalenka upset. She said the director cares more about money than the players' well-being. Sabalenka might not play in Dubai again. Other players, like Coco Gauff, support Sabalenka and think the calendar is too busy, causing injuries. Tennis players often talk about having too many games in a year. In other news, Katie Boulter won her match in Miami.

articles.title: A Robot That Picks Tomatoes Smartly
articles.summary: Scientists have made a robot that can pick tomatoes better. Tomatoes grow in bunches, so it's hard for robots to choose only the ripe ones. A professor named Takuya Fujinaga from Osaka Metropolitan University created a system to help robots decide which tomatoes are easy to pick. The robot looks at pictures of tomatoes, their stems, and if leaves are in the way. It uses this information to pick tomatoes from the best angle. In tests, the robot was successful 81% of the time. Sometimes, if it couldn't pick a tomato from the front, it tried from the side and succeeded. This helps farmers because there aren't enough workers. In the future, robots might pick easy tomatoes while people handle the tricky ones, working together to grow food.

articles.title: Nine Cities Compete to Be UK City of Culture 2029
articles.summary: Nine cities in the UK are trying to become the City of Culture for 2029. They are Blackpool, Inverness and the Highlands, Ipswich, Middlesbrough, Milton Keynes, Portsmouth, Sheffield, Swindon, and Wrexham. The government chose them and gave each city £60,000 to make a plan. The winner will get £10 million to do fun cultural activities for a year. The next three best cities will get £125,000 each. The Culture Secretary, Lisa Nandy, said this helps share opportunities across the country. A writer named Sir Phil Redmond leads the group that picks the winner. He says culture brings people together and makes cities proud. Past winners like Coventry made lots of money and had more visitors. This competition is exciting for everyone!

articles.title: A Strange Sulfur Planet Found by a Space Telescope
articles.summary: Scientists using the James Webb Space Telescope have found a weird planet far away from Earth. This planet, called L 98-59 d, orbits a red star about 35 light-years away. It is bigger than Earth but has low density and an atmosphere with hydrogen sulfide, which smells like rotten eggs. Unlike other planets, it doesn't fit into known categories. Researchers think it has a deep ocean of molten rock inside, called a magma ocean, that traps lots of sulfur. This magma ocean helps keep a thick atmosphere with sulfur gases. The planet is not like Earth and probably cannot support life, but it shows there are many different kinds of planets in space. This discovery helps scientists learn more about planets beyond our Solar System.
